title: Understanding Angular Momentum
text: Angular momentum \( \vec{L} \) of a particle is given by the cross product of its position vector \( \vec{r} \) and its linear momentum \( \vec{p} \).

title: Calculate Linear Momentum
text: The linear momentum \( \vec{p} \) of a particle is calculated as the product of its mass \( $$m$$ \) and velocity \( \vec{v} \).

title: Compute Linear Momentum
text: Given the mass \( $$m=5.0$$ \) kg and velocity \( $$\vec{v}=3.0\hat{i$$} \) $$\frac{m}{s}$$, calculate the linear momentum \( \vec{p} \).

title: Cross Product Calculation
text: The cross product \( \vec{r} \times \vec{p} \) is calculated using the determinant of a matrix formed by the unit vectors \( \hat{i}, \hat{j}, \hat{k} \), and the components of \( \vec{r} \) and \( \vec{p} \).
